Title: TRUECAM: Trust in NSCLC Diagnosis with a Conformalized Uncertainty-Aware Framework
Covariate / label shift handled?: Partial -- empirical only; authors state coverage cannot be guaranteed in deployment because validity is restored by detecting-and-removing OOD inputs, not by a guarantee that provably survives in-scope covariate shift
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Single disease/modality (NSCLC histopathology), single cohort-pair (TCGA<->CPTAC); shift-validity is empirical via OOD removal, not a maintained distribution-free guarantee under covariate shift; not yet peer-reviewed
Reusable asset (code/split/score): Code (github.com/iamownt/TRUECAM); TCGA<->CPTAC cross-cohort split; SNGP OOD-detector design; CRC calibration recipe

Title: Conformal Triage for Medical Imaging AI Deployment
Covariate / label shift handled?: Partial -- shift handled by recalibrating on local target labels (not a guarantee that travels without labeled target data)
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: No far-OOD routing at all; shift addressed by recalibration requiring labeled target data, not weighting/adaptation; private single-site (MGB) evaluation, not public or multi-dataset
Reusable asset (code/split/score): PPV/NPV-targeted conformal triage procedure (re-derivable); no public split or code released

Title: Conformal selective prediction with cost-aware deferral for safe clinical triage under distribution shift (Kim)
Covariate / label shift handled?: Partial -- weighted CP degrades gracefully under a single temporal shift split rather than maintaining the guarantee
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: No far-OOD module; one EHR task (sepsis) only, single temporal train/test split, not imaging or multi-dataset; coverage is empirically graceful, not guaranteed, under shift
Reusable asset (code/split/score): Weighted/Mondrian conformal deferral procedure; PhysioNet 2019 sepsis split (public, reusable benchmark)

Title: Audited Conformal Prediction under Unknown Distribution Shift
Abstains / defers?: Partial -- produces prediction sets, not a native abstain/defer decision
Covariate / label shift handled?: Yes (CAMELYON17, but requires labeled target data)
OOD handling: No -- covariate shift only; no far-OOD component
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: No native deferral mechanism (sets only); needs labeled target data to audit/correct coverage; handles covariate shift only, not far-OOD; single benchmark
Reusable asset (code/split/score): CAMELYON17-WILDS evaluation split; audited-coverage correction procedure

Title: Selective Classification Under Distribution Shifts (Liang and Sun)
Distribution-free property: No -- confidence-score method with no distribution-free guarantee
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: No distribution-free guarantee of any kind (risk-coverage curves only, not a coverage/risk theorem); evaluated on ImageNet/Amazon-WILDS, not medical data
Reusable asset (code/split/score): Confidence-score construction for joint shift+OOD selective classification; ImageNet/Amazon-WILDS shift splits

Title: Selective Conformal Risk Control (SCRC)
Covariate / label shift handled?: No -- guarantees assume exchangeability; SCRC-T even pools calibration and test
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Guarantee assumes exchangeability outright (no shift mechanism at all); no far-OOD routing; evaluated on two general public datasets, not medical shift -- this is the closest distribution-free-guarantee ancestor for the selection step, but shift-free
Reusable asset (code/split/score): SCRC-T / SCRC-I select-then-control proof structure (see summary below); general-purpose, reusable on any score+selection setup

Title: Conformal Selective Prediction with General Risk Control (SCoRE)
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Same gap as SCRC: e-value risk control is exchangeability-only; no shift or OOD handling; no medical validation
Reusable asset (code/split/score): e-value risk-control construction (alternative proof route to SCRC, worth comparing in design doc)

Title: Pitfalls of Conformal Predictions for Medical Image Classification
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Not a method -- demonstrates conformal coverage is unreliable under real medical input/label shift and argues conformal should not be used for selective classification as-is; used here as motivating evidence that the gap is open, not as a competitor to out-perform
Reusable asset (code/split/score): None (critique paper); cite as motivation in positioning memo intro

Title: A Deployment Audit of Release-Side Risk in Conformal Triage under Prevalence Shift
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Not a method -- shows conformal-triage guarantees (e.g. Conformal Triage above) can break under prevalence shift specifically; used as motivating evidence, not a competitor row to beat
Reusable asset (code/split/score): None (critique paper); cite alongside the Pitfalls paper as motivation

Title: Conformal Prediction Under Covariate Shift (Tibshirani, Foygel Barber, Candes, Ramdas)
Abstains / defers?: No -- not selective; issues an interval/set for every input
Covariate / label shift handled?: Yes (covariate shift, with a known/estimable likelihood ratio)
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Foundational weighted-conformal machinery, but no abstention mechanism, no far-OOD component, and never validated on medical shift data -- a component this pipeline composes with selection, not a competitor
Reusable asset (code/split/score): weighted_conformal_threshold construction verified against ryantibs/conformal in 0.1.5; density-ratio-via-discriminator estimation recipe

Title: Adaptive Conformal Inference Under Distribution Shift (Gibbs and Candes)
Abstains / defers?: No -- not selective; online interval update, no abstain decision
Covariate / label shift handled?: Yes (arbitrary online drift, via label feedback)
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Foundational online-shift machinery; not selective, not far-OOD-aware, not validated on medical shift data, and the guarantee is long-run/amortized rather than per-case finite-sample
Reusable asset (code/split/score): alpha_t update rule verified against herbps10/AdaptiveConformal in 0.1.5; reserved for the temporal/continual shift track (2.2a), not the core MVN

Title: SelectiveNet: Integrated Reject Option
Distribution-free property: No -- risk control is in-distribution/PAC under the i.i.d. assumption only
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: No distribution-free guarantee under shift of any kind; pure in-distribution abstention; no far-OOD handling -- serves only as the strong-baseline abstention head in the seed-baseline comparison (0.3 step's baseline list)
Reusable asset (code/split/score): Joint train-time selection-head architecture; reusable as a UQ-score baseline (softmax-response variant) against this pipeline

Title: Deep Gamblers: Learning to Abstain with Portfolio Theory
Distribution-free property: No -- same family as SelectiveNet, abstention only, no guarantee
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Same gap as SelectiveNet: abstention mechanism with no distribution-free guarantee and no shift/OOD handling; included only as an alternative abstention-score baseline
Reusable asset (code/split/score): Portfolio-theory abstention training objective; reusable as a second abstention-score baseline

Title: Deep ensembles / MC-dropout as UQ-based abstention (Lakshminarayanan 2017; Gal and Ghahramani 2016)
Abstains / defers?: Partial -- thresholded abstention possible but not a native mechanism
Distribution-free property: No -- no distribution-free guarantee; calibration is a heuristic
Covariate / label shift handled?: No -- calibration is known to degrade under shift (Ovadia et al. 2019)
OOD handling: Partial -- ensemble disagreement can heuristically flag far-OOD but with no error control
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: No guarantee at any stage; calibration degrades under shift (the well-documented Ovadia et al. 2019 finding); OOD flagging is heuristic, uncontrolled -- the strong classical UQ baseline this pipeline must beat on both coverage AND abstention quality
Reusable asset (code/split/score): Ensemble/MC-dropout uncertainty score as a baseline scoring function feeding the selection rule g(x)=1[u(x)<=tau]

Title: Mahalanobis / Energy / kNN OOD detection (Lee 2018; Liu 2020; Sun 2022)
Abstains / defers?: Partial -- flag/route only, not an abstention policy with cost structure
Distribution-free property: No -- no selective coverage or risk-control guarantee
Covariate / label shift handled?: No -- not a selective-prediction method at all
OOD handling: Yes -- this is the (4) component itself
Open limitation (its assumption / data /: Pure far-OOD detection scores, no selective coverage or risk guarantee attached -- exactly the OOD building block this pipeline integrates, not a competing end-to-end method
Reusable asset (code/split/score): Mahalanobis/kNN scoring functions verified against mueller-mp/maha-norm and deeplearning-wisc/knn-ood; reusable as-is as this pipeline's OOD component
